rationale: This change adds a minimal in-memory token-bucket rate limiter under examples/token_bucket.py to serve as a reference implementation. The author chose a lazy-refill design (tokens are replenished on demand at check time rather than by a background timer) to keep the implementation simple and dependency-free. State is stored per user-ID key in a plain dict; the author confirmed key cardinality is in the low-thousands range, so unbounded dict growth is acceptable for this stub, with the explicit expectation that any production variant would add TTL or LRU eviction. The single-process constraint is deliberate: this is a scoped reference example, not a general-purpose library component. Multi-replica deployments would require shared state (e.g. Redis-backed) and are explicitly out of scope here. The primary risks are: (1) unbounded _state growth if deployed as-is with high-cardinality keys — mitigated by the examples/ placement and docstring warning; (2) per-process-only semantics being misapplied in a multi-process environment — mitigated by the explicit 'Single-process only' docstring callout and the examples/ location signaling non-library status; (3) no rollback concern since this is a net-new file with no callers — removing it is a clean revert.
